type.prompt:
# PLAN.md — Amelia Boone Pre-Call Sprint **Night Owl Studio, LLC** · Client: Amelia Boone **Repo**: `https://github.com/nightowlstudiollc/amelia-boone` **Preview**: `https://amelia-boone-archive.netlify.app/` **Call**: Today, Wednesday March 11, 2026 at 4pm PT **Current time**: Morning of the 11th — approximately 4–6 hours of runway --- ## Current State The demo site is live and functional. Amelia has seen it and said "I love it - looks like exactly what I need." The call today is a closing / scoping conversation, not a pitch. **Already built:** - Astro 5 + AstroPaper theme, dark minimal design - ~13 representative WordPress posts in `src/data/blog/` - ~20 Substack posts in `src/data/blog/` (prefixed `substack-`) - About page (`src/pages/about.md`) — written, good quality - Contact page stub, Search (Pagefind), RSS, tags, archives — all functional - Netlify auto-deploy on push to main **Missing that matters for the call:** 1. Full WordPress archive — only ~13 of ~125 posts imported 2. Automated Substack sync — demo is a static snapshot; new posts don't appear automatically 3. CLAUDE.md has wrong content path (`src/content/blog/` → actual is `src/data/blog/`) --- ## Tech Notes for CCCLI ### Content Directory Posts live in **`src/data/blog/`** — not `src/content/blog/` as the old CLAUDE.md says. ### Frontmatter Schema (exact, from actual existing posts) ```yaml --- title: "Post Title" pubDatetime: 2018-04-02T12:00:00Z # ISO 8601, always include time component description: "One-sentence summary, ~150 chars" source: wordpress # or: substack originalUrl: "https://..." tags: - racing # lowercase, hyphenated, 1-3 per post draft: false featured: false # only true for ~4-5 top posts total --- ``` Key: field is `pubDatetime` NOT `date`. `draft: false` always. ### Stack - Package manager: **pnpm** (not npm) - Build: `pnpm run build` → `astro check && astro build && pagefind --site dist && cp -r dist/pagefind public/` - Dev: `pnpm run dev` - Node: 20 ### GitHub Auth (PAT for nightowlstudiollc org) ``` 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 ``` Use Bearer format. Headers: `Authorization: Bearer {TOKEN}`, `Accept: application/vnd.github+json`, `X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2022-11-28`, `User-Agent: kebab-tax-bot`. --- ## Task 1 — Full WordPress Import ★ CRITICAL, DO FIRST ★ **Goal:** Import all ~125 WP posts from `ameliabooneracing.com` into `src/data/blog/`. Currently only ~13 are present. ### 1A — Fetch all posts via WP REST API (paginated) ```bash # Page 1 curl "https://www.ameliabooneracing.com/wp-json/wp/v2/posts?per_page=100&page=1&_fields=id,date,title,content,slug,categories,tags,excerpt,link" # Page 2 (continue until empty array returned) curl "https://www.ameliabooneracing.com/wp-json/wp/v2/posts?per_page=100&page=2&_fields=id,date,title,content,slug,categories,tags,excerpt,link" ``` Fields to use: - `date` → `pubDatetime` (append `T12:00:00Z` if no time component) - `title.rendered` → `title` (unescape HTML entities) - `content.rendered` → body (convert HTML → Markdown, see 1C) - `slug` → filename `{slug}.md` - `excerpt.rendered` → `description` (strip HTML, truncate to 150 chars) - `link` → `originalUrl` - `categories` → tags (resolve via 1B, or default to `[racing]`) ### 1B — Resolve category/tag IDs (optional, do if fast) ```bash curl "https://www.ameliabooneracing.com/wp-json/wp/v2/categories?per_page=100" curl "https://www.ameliabooneracing.com/wp-json/wp/v2/tags?per_page=100" ``` Suggested tag mapping (keep it to ~6 values max): - `ultramarathons` → `ultrarunning` - `obstacle-racing` or `ocr` → `racing` - `personal`, `life`, `thoughts` → `essays` - `injury`, `recovery`, `health` → `recovery` - `training` → `training` If taxonomy resolution is slow or complex, just default all WP posts to `tags: [racing]` and move on. Import completeness matters more than taxonomy. ### 1C — Convert HTML → Markdown ```bash pip install html2text ``` ```python import html2text, re h = html2text.HTML2Text() h.ignore_links = False h.body_width = 0 h.ignore_images = False def html_to_md(html): md = h.handle(html) # Strip WP shortcodes md = re.sub(r'\[caption[^\]]*\].*?\[/caption\]', '', md, flags=re.DOTALL) md = re.sub(r'\[gallery[^\]]*\]', '', md) md = re.sub(r'\[embed\].*?\[/embed\]', '', md, flags=re.DOTALL) md = re.sub(r'\[/?[a-z_]+[^\]]*\]', '', md) # catch-all for remaining shortcodes # Fix common WP entities md = md.replace(' ', ' ').replace('', '') md = re.sub(r'\n{3,}', '\n\n', md) return md.strip() ``` ### 1D — Write files (skip existing) ```python import os def write_wp_post(slug, title, pubDatetime, description, body, originalUrl, tags): path = f"src/data/blog/{slug}.md" if os.path.exists(path): print(f"SKIP (exists): {slug}") return t = title.replace('"', '\\"').replace('\n', ' ').strip() d = description[:148].replace('"', '\\"').replace('\n', ' ').strip() tags_yaml = "\n".join(f" - {tag}" for tag in tags) content = f"""--- title: "{t}" pubDatetime: {pubDatetime} description: "{d}" source: wordpress originalUrl: "{originalUrl}" tags: {tags_yaml} draft: false featured: false --- {body} """ with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f: f.write(content) print(f"WROTE: {path}") ``` ### 1E — Set featured on key posts after import After the bulk write, set `featured: true` in frontmatter for these (edit the files directly): - `how-worlds-toughest-mudder-ruined-my-life.md` (already in repo — check if already featured) - `barkley2018.md` - `the-first-dnf.md` - One high-resonance injury/recovery/identity post from the new batch Cap at 4-5 total featured across all posts. Too many breaks the AstroPaper homepage grid. ### 1F — Commit ```bash git add src/data/blog/ git commit -m "feat: full WordPress archive import (~125 posts)" git push origin main ``` Netlify builds in ~2-3 min. Verify at https://amelia-boone-archive.netlify.app/posts/ that the full archive appears. --- ## Task 2 — Automated Substack Sync via Netlify Scheduled Function **Goal:** Build a function that polls the Substack RSS feed hourly and commits new posts to the repo via GitHub API. This triggers an automatic Netlify rebuild. **Why commit-based**: Netlify scheduled functions are serverless — no filesystem access. Writing to GitHub is the correct pattern; each new post triggers a build naturally. ### 2A — Create `netlify/functions/sync-substack.mts` ```typescript import type { Config } from "@netlify/functions"; import Parser from "rss-parser"; import { Octokit } from "@octokit/rest"; const REPO_OWNER = "nightowlstudiollc"; const REPO_NAME = "amelia-boone"; const CONTENT_PATH = "src/data/blog"; export default async function handler() { const GITHUB_TOKEN = process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN; if (!GITHUB_TOKEN) throw new Error("GITHUB_TOKEN env var not set"); const octokit = new Octokit({ auth: GITHUB_TOKEN }); const parser = new Parser(); const feed = await parser.parseURL("https://ameliaboone.substack.com/feed"); const { data: existingFiles } = await octokit.repos.getContent({ owner: REPO_OWNER, repo: REPO_NAME, path: CONTENT_PATH, }); const existingSlugs = new Set( (existingFiles as any[]) .filter(f => f.name.startsWith("substack-")) .map(f => f.name.replace(".md", "")) ); let synced = 0; for (const item of feed.items) { const urlSlug = item.link?.split("/p/")[1]?.replace(/\/$/, "") ?? ""; if (!urlSlug) continue; const filename = `substack-${urlSlug}`; if (existingSlugs.has(filename)) continue; const fullContent = (item as any)["content:encoded"] ?? item.content ?? item.summary ?? ""; const markdownBody = htmlToMarkdown(fullContent); const pubDatetime = new Date(item.pubDate || item.isoDate || Date.now()) .toISOString().replace(/\.\d{3}Z$/, "Z"); const description = stripHtml(item.summary || item.contentSnippet || "") .substring(0, 148).trim().replace(/"/g, '\\"'); const title = (item.title || "").replace(/"/g, '\\"'); const fileContent = `--- title: "${title}" pubDatetime: ${pubDatetime} description: "${description}" source: substack originalUrl: "${item.link || ""}" tags: - essays draft: false featured: false --- ${markdownBody} `; await octokit.repos.createOrUpdateFileContents({ owner: REPO_OWNER, repo: REPO_NAME, path: `${CONTENT_PATH}/${filename}.md`, message: `chore: sync Substack post "${item.title}"`, content: Buffer.from(fileContent).toString("base64"), }); console.log(`Synced: ${filename}`); synced++; } return new Response(`Sync complete. ${synced} new posts.`, { status: 200 }); } function stripHtml(html: string): string { return html.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, "").replace(/\s+/g, " ").trim(); } function htmlToMarkdown(html: string): string { return html .replace(/]*>(.*?)<\/h1>/gi, "# $1\n\n") .replace(/]*>(.*?)<\/h2>/gi, "## $1\n\n") .replace(/]*>(.*?)<\/h3>/gi, "### $1\n\n") .replace(/]*>(.*?)<\/p>/gis, "$1\n\n") .replace(/]*>(.*?)<\/strong>/gi, "**$1**") .replace(/]*>(.*?)<\/em>/gi, "*$1*") .replace(/]*href="([^"]*)"[^>]*>(.*?)<\/a>/gi, "[$2]($1)") .replace(/]*src="([^"]*)"[^>]*alt="([^"]*)"[^>]*\/?>/gi, "![$2]($1)") .replace(/]*src="([^"]*)"[^>]*\/?>/gi, "![]($1)") .replace(//gi, "\n") .replace(/<[^>]+>/g, "") .replace(/ /g, " ").replace(/&/g, "&").replace(/</g, "<") .replace(/>/g, ">").replace(/"/g, '"') .replace(/…/g, "…").replace(/“/g, "\u201C").replace(/”/g, "\u201D") .replace(/\n{3,}/g, "\n\n").trim(); } export const config: Config = { schedule: "@hourly", }; ``` ### 2B — Add dependencies ```bash pnpm add rss-parser @octokit/rest pnpm add -D @netlify/functions ``` ### 2C — Update netlify.toml Add to existing file (don't replace the build block, just append): ```toml [functions] node_bundler = "esbuild" ``` ### 2D — GITHUB_TOKEN (Andrew must do manually) **CCCLI cannot set Netlify env vars.** Andrew must: 1. Netlify dashboard → `amelia-boone-archive` → Site configuration → Environment variables 2. Add variable: `GITHUB_TOKEN` = the nightowlstudiollc PAT from the project auth file Leave a visible TODO in the commit message and in a comment at the top of the function file. ### 2E — Commit ```bash git add netlify/ netlify.toml package.json pnpm-lock.yaml git commit -m "feat: Netlify scheduled function for hourly Substack sync Polls ameliaboone.substack.com/feed hourly. New posts committed to src/data/blog/ via GitHub API, triggering rebuild. TODO (Andrew): Set GITHUB_TOKEN in Netlify environment variables. Netlify UI > amelia-boone-archive > Site configuration > Environment variables" git push origin main ``` --- ## Task 3 — Fix CLAUDE.md (5 minutes) Update the existing `CLAUDE.md` in the repo: 1. Content path: `src/content/blog/` → `src/data/blog/` everywhere 2. Package manager note: pnpm, not npm 3. Frontmatter: `pubDatetime` not `date` 4. Note that Pagefind is automatic in the build — don't run it separately 5. Update "Current Status" section to reflect full WP import and scheduled sync ```bash git add CLAUDE.md git commit -m "docs: fix CLAUDE.md content path and schema accuracy" git push origin main ``` --- ## Task 4 — Verify Existing Substack Posts (10 minutes) Quick sanity check on the ~20 existing Substack files: ```bash # Check frontmatter of first few ls src/data/blog/substack-*.md | head -5 | xargs -I{} head -15 {} # Check for HTML leakage grep -l "

" src/data/blog/substack-*.md grep -l "&" src/data/blog/substack-*.md ``` Fix any malformed files. Common issues: - Raw `

` or `
` tags in body - `&`, ` ` not converted - `pubDatetime` missing time component (add `T12:00:00Z`) - Empty `description` field --- ## Task 5 — Homepage Featured Posts (10 minutes) `postPerIndex: 4` in `src/config.ts` — ensure exactly 4 posts have `featured: true`. Check current state: ```bash grep -rl "featured: true" src/data/blog/ ``` Target lineup (adjust based on what's in the archive after Task 1): 1. Most recent Substack post by date 2. `substack-so-this-is-40.md` or another personal milestone Substack essay 3. `how-worlds-toughest-mudder-ruined-my-life.md` 4. A strong WP post from the newly imported batch (injury/identity theme) Do not set more than 5 total. AstroPaper homepage hero breaks with too many featured posts. --- ## Post-Call Tasks (Do NOT start before 4pm) These are build targets for after she signs — or can be demoed verbally if she asks. **Substack Refresh Button (+$100 add-on):** A `/admin/refresh` page with a button that POSTs to trigger the sync function on demand, protected by a secret URL param. Useful if she wants to sync immediately after posting rather than waiting for the hourly cron. **Direct Editing (+$100 add-on):** `editPost.enabled: true` in `src/config.ts` links each post to its GitHub edit URL. Fine for Markdown-comfortable users. If she wants a visual editor, scope Decap CMS separately — it's free and integrates with Netlify/GitHub but requires more setup. --- ## Call Talking Points (Andrew reads before 4pm — not CCCLI tasks) **Domain situation — options ranked:** 1. **Informal ask** — Ask her ex directly. Free and fast if the relationship allows it. 2. **New domain + 301 redirect** — `ameliaboone.com`, `ameliaboonewriting.com`, or `raceipsaloquitur.com` may be available. If she can get any DNS access to the old host, a 301 preserves most SEO equity. Cleanest option if informal ask fails. 3. **GoDaddy registrant recovery** — Requires proof of identity/business relationship. Days to weeks, cooperation not guaranteed. 4. **Stay on Netlify subdomain** — `amelia-boone-archive.netlify.app` works fine as a working URL while the domain question is resolved. Not precious about it. 5. **ICANN UDRP** — Only for hostile bad actors. Not applicable here. **What to get from her on the call:** - WordPress admin credentials (needed for redirect mapping — old URLs should 301 to new site's URL structure once live) - Import preference: all 125 WP posts, or does she want to curate? Default plan is all of them. - Substack paywall preference: RSS only imports free posts. Is that acceptable, or does she want paywalled posts shown with a link-through to Substack? - Any design feedback from time with the demo - Pricing confirmation ($650 base, add-ons as discussed in proposal) - Timeline: target date? Any urgency? **What not to promise:** - Domain resolution timing (not in our control) - WordPress comment migration (complex, probably not worth it — Substack has no comments either) - Specific launch dates before you have her WP credentials and know her timeline --- ## Priority Order If running short on time, stop at whichever task you reach: | Priority | Task | Time Estimate | |----------|------|--------------| | 1 ★ | Full WordPress import | 60-90 min | | 2 | Fix CLAUDE.md | 5 min | | 3 | Verify Substack posts | 10 min | | 4 | Featured post selection | 10 min | | 5 | Substack sync function | 30-60 min | The site closes the deal at current state. Task 1 makes it dramatically better. Everything else is polish. Deployed and imperfect beats perfect and local.
